steps.title: Spot the core requirement
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference in the origins of the two words. To accomplish this, the answer must name both origins explicitly and show a contrast between them.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A explicitly states both origins—”guerdon” from Anglo-French and “Laodicean” from ancient Greek—using “while” to emphasize the contrast between them.

Choices B, C, and D either omit the origins entirely, cover only one word's origin, or mention both words without specifying their distinct origins.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
